biomcp_log.q: NCCN ATM germline prostate cancer PSA screening guideline
ATM heterozygous colorectal cancer surveillance colonoscopy NCCN
KDIGO 2024 cystatin C eGFR confirmation guideline
ATM male breast cancer surveillance NCCN guideline
PHILharmonic IMPACT ATM prostate PSA screening BRCA
ACC AHA 2018 cholesterol guideline ApoB primary prevention
USPSTF hepatitis C screening adults 2020 grade B
Inker cystatin C creatinine eGFR equation muscle mass bias
IMPACT study prostate cancer screening BRCA2 ATM PSA early detection
colonoscopy screening average risk meta-analysis mortality reduction
hs-CRP cardiovascular risk reclassification ACC AHA primary prevention
Grundy 2018 ACC AHA cholesterol guideline risk enhancers
Page IMPACT BRCA2 prostate cancer screening MRI biopsy 2019
USPSTF lung cancer screening 2021 low-dose CT eligibility criteria

register_pinned_actions.action_name: Genetic counseling + cascade testing for ATM heterozygous P/LP variant
register_pinned_actions.guideline_basis: ACMG SF v3.2 (Miller 2022) · NCCN Genetic/Familial High-Risk v2.2024
register_pinned_actions.intervention: Refer to clinical geneticist for ATM c.8147T>C heterozygous P/LP confirmation, counseling, and cascade testing of first-degree relatives.
register_pinned_actions.mechanism: P/LP variant on ACMG Secondary Findings list. Counseling + gene-specific surveillance + cascade testing are guideline-mandated for actionable hereditary cancer predisposition.
register_pinned_actions.tracking_metric: Geneticist visit completed; cascade offer documented for 1° relatives

citations.supports: ACMG SF v3.2 secondary findings list including ATM
CARRIERS — ATM cancer ORs supporting cascade rationale

register_pinned_actions.action_name: Annual pancreatic surveillance per CAPS 2020 — start age 50
register_pinned_actions.guideline_basis: International CAPS Consortium 2020 (Goggins et al.) · NCCN pancreas v1.2024
register_pinned_actions.intervention: Document plan for annual MRI/MRCP + EUS starting age 50.
register_pinned_actions.mechanism: ATM het + 2° relative with PDAC meets CAPS surveillance criteria. ATM ~5–9x lifetime PDAC risk; stage-shift detection via imaging is the dominant mortality lever.
register_pinned_actions.tracking_metric: Plan in chart; first imaging scheduled at age 50

soc_ranked_candidates.action_name: Average-risk colorectal cancer screening — initiate now (age 47)

trigger.summary: Patient is 47 (eligible 45–75); ATM het + paternal-line GI cancer further raises pre-test probability.

soc_ranked_candidates.guideline_anchor: USPSTF Grade B 2021 (CRC screening 45–75); NCCN CRC Screening v2.2024 (ATM = consider screening at 40 or 10y before earliest dx, q5y colonoscopy)
soc_ranked_candidates.mechanism: Colonoscopy detects and removes adenomatous precursors and identifies early-stage CRC. ATM het carries ~1.5–2x CRC OR; paternal-line GI cancer further compresses screening interval per NCCN.
soc_ranked_candidates.intervention: Screening colonoscopy now; if normal, repeat q5y per NCCN ATM-het pathway (vs q10y average-risk). FIT acceptable interim option.
soc_ranked_candidates.tracking_metric: Colonoscopy completed; pathology documented; surveillance interval set in chart
soc_ranked_candidates.urgency: within 3 months (overdue under USPSTF; ATM-modified interval q5y)

spareq.s_reason: ATM het + family GI hx + age ≥45 — strong, multi-source signal
spareq.p_reason: USPSTF + NCCN + multi-society endorsement; high-prior intervention
spareq.a_reason: Outcome-redirect: polypectomy prevents incident CRC; stage-shift on detection
spareq.r_reason: Single bowel prep + procedure; covered by insurance; widely available
spareq.q_reason: USPSTF Grade B + NCCN + multiple RCT/cohort evidence for mortality reduction

citations.supports: Hereditary CRC including ATM-modified surveillance pathways

soc_ranked_candidates.action_name: Baseline PSA for ATM-carrier prostate cancer screening

trigger.summary: ATM het ~2x prostate OR; NCCN recommends earlier-onset PSA discussion for ATM/BRCA carriers at 40+.

soc_ranked_candidates.guideline_anchor: NCCN Prostate Cancer Early Detection v1.2024 (ATM/BRCA carriers — consider baseline PSA at age 40, annual thereafter); Philadelphia Prostate Cancer Consensus 2019
soc_ranked_candidates.mechanism: ATM-deficient prostate cancers occur earlier and trend more aggressive. Baseline PSA at 40+ in carriers enables velocity tracking and earlier biopsy threshold; supported by IMPACT study cohort data in BRCA2/ATM carriers.
soc_ranked_candidates.intervention: Serum PSA now; if <1.0 ng/mL repeat annually, if ≥1.0 ng/mL annual + urology referral threshold lowered.
soc_ranked_candidates.tracking_metric: PSA result on file; annual cadence established
soc_ranked_candidates.evoi_reasoning: Result changes management: triggers urology referral / MRI pathway if elevated; establishes baseline velocity reference even if low.

spareq.s_reason: ATM P/LP carrier age 47 — direct guideline trigger
spareq.p_reason: NCCN-anchored carrier pathway; less universal than population PSA debate
spareq.a_reason: Stage-shift potential for ATM-associated prostate cancer; outcome-redirect via earlier biopsy threshold
spareq.r_reason: Single venipuncture, low cost, included in standard chem panels
spareq.e_reason: No procedural harm; downstream overdiagnosis risk mitigated by carrier-specific pathway
spareq.q_reason: Single-society (NCCN) carrier-pathway recommendation + Philadelphia consensus + IMPACT cohort

citations.supports: Guidelines for genetic testing and PSA screening in prostate cancer (Philadelphia consensus + NCCN review)
Hereditary urologic cancer surveillance including ATM PSA pathway
Multidisciplinary consensus on germline-driven prostate screening

trigger.summary: eGFR 72 with Cr 1.25 in muscular phenotype likely overestimates CKD risk; KDIGO 2024 recommends cystatin C confirmation when creatinine-based eGFR is discordant with clinical context.

soc_ranked_candidates.guideline_anchor: KDIGO 2024 CKD Evaluation & Management Guideline (cystatin C confirmation for borderline / discordant eGFR)
soc_ranked_candidates.mechanism: Cystatin C is independent of muscle mass; combined creatinine–cystatin C eGFR (eGFRcr-cys) reduces bias in athletic/high-muscle phenotypes and reclassifies many borderline-Stage 2 patients to true GFR ≥90.
soc_ranked_candidates.intervention: Order serum cystatin C; calculate eGFRcr-cys (CKD-EPI 2021).
soc_ranked_candidates.tracking_metric: eGFRcr-cys result documented; CKD staging finalized
soc_ranked_candidates.evoi_reasoning: Result resolves whether eGFR 72 represents true mild reduction vs muscle-mass artifact; changes whether annual eGFR/UACR monitoring vs no further follow-up is indicated.

spareq.s_reason: Single discordant signal (eGFR 72 + Cr 1.25 high-normal) in muscular phenotype
spareq.p_reason: KDIGO-recommended confirmatory test in this exact scenario
spareq.a_reason: Trajectory-anchor: avoids mislabeling as Stage 2 CKD; redirects monitoring intensity
spareq.r_reason: Single add-on serum test, low cost
spareq.q_reason: Single guideline body (KDIGO) + multiple cohort/validation studies

citations.supports: Cystatin C as GFR estimation marker — systematic review
Comparison of creatinine, cystatin C, and combined equations in real-world CKD
Accuracy of GFR estimates — cystatin C reduces bias

trigger.summary: hs-CRP not in panel; ACC/AHA 2018/2019 lists hs-CRP ≥2.0 mg/L as a risk-enhancing factor that informs borderline statin decisions.

soc_ranked_candidates.guideline_anchor: ACC/AHA 2018 Cholesterol Guideline + 2019 Primary Prevention Guideline (hs-CRP listed risk enhancer; Class IIb)
soc_ranked_candidates.mechanism: hs-CRP reflects residual inflammatory risk; in this patient with CAC=0 and ApoB borderline, an elevated hs-CRP would shift the borderline ApoB conversation, while a low hs-CRP further supports continued statin deferral.
soc_ranked_candidates.intervention: Add hs-CRP to next fasting lipid panel.
soc_ranked_candidates.tracking_metric: hs-CRP value documented; lipid management plan updated
soc_ranked_candidates.evoi_reasoning: In a CAC=0 patient with borderline ApoB 96 and LDL 121, hs-CRP is the lowest-cost risk-enhancer that meaningfully informs whether to revisit statin initiation before the CAC warranty expires (~5–7y).

spareq.s_reason: Borderline ApoB + LDL in patient with CAC=0; missing risk-enhancer
spareq.p_reason: Guideline-listed enhancer; impact moderated by CAC=0 protective context
spareq.a_reason: Trajectory-anchor: low likelihood of changing immediate management given CAC=0 power-of-zero
spareq.q_reason: ACC/AHA 2018/2019 Class IIb risk enhancer; supportive cohort evidence

citations.supports: ASPC clinical practice statement reaffirming hs-CRP as ACC/AHA risk enhancer
Critical appraisal of lipid management — biomarker-enhanced risk stratification
Integrating biomarkers including hs-CRP into ASCVD risk stratification

considered_and_excluded.reason: no_signal — PREVENT very-low-risk band, CAC=0 power-of-zero, BP 116/76, A1c 5.3, optimal Lp(a); ACC/AHA 2018 supports deferral. Revisit at CAC warranty expiration.

considered_and_excluded.action_name: Annual breast MRI for ATM male carrier
considered_and_excluded.reason: contraindicated — NCCN does not recommend male breast MRI screening for ATM carriers; clinical breast exam education only.
no_signal — patient does not meet USPSTF 2021 eligibility (no ≥20 pack-year smoking history).

covered_by_register_pin: separate routine universal screen; if not yet completed should be ordered at next primary-care visit per USPSTF Grade B 2020 — flagged for PCP routine but not load-bearing here.
no_signal — age 47 male, no risk factors; below USPSTF/Endocrine Society thresholds.
belongs_in_adjuncts_bucket — no guideline body endorses APOE testing for asymptomatic primary-prevention neurodegeneration risk; informative but not SoC.
contraindicated — USPSTF 2022 recommends against initiating aspirin for primary ASCVD prevention in adults ≥40 absent clear high-risk indication; this patient is very-low-risk band.
covered_by_register_pin: bundled with S3 follow-up — KDIGO recommends UACR alongside eGFR; will be ordered concurrent with cystatin C, not a separate scored candidate.
